intro: Schwantesia proxima, also known as the 'Red Hottentot's Fig', is a succulent plant species belonging to the Aizoaceae family, native to the deserts and semi-arid regions of southern Africa. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Schwantesia proxima include flavonoids, alkaloids, and triterpenoid saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Schwantesia proxima is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and topical ointments, as well as being incorporated into teas and tinctures.
